creator: kib@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Kirk McKusick from comment #19)
You only loose usermode access to the console.

Imagine that somebody want to unmount and then mount /dev again, for whatever reasons, e.g. as part of more involved re-rooting.  I do not see why should we prevent this.  For the same reason, we do not prevent ifconfig down the interface which was used for nfs boot.
